title: Make demo3 A-B loop checkbox a real enable or disable control
summary: 0.6.47 gives the demo3 A-B loop checkbox a real action path so it can disable and re-enable an existing valid range, and it keeps the checkbox disabled until a valid A-B span exists for the current video.
changes: Demo3 A-B loop toggles now fire `Player A-B Enable` when checked and `Player A-B Disable` when unchecked.
The runtime now disables the demo3 A-B loop toggle until the current player has a valid A-B range.
The runtime still clears and disables the A-B loop toggle when navigation or clear removes the current range.
reasoning: The scene-builder and bool-backed toggle readback seam were already correct, so the next narrow step was to let the checkbox actually control A-B loop enable state.
A-B needs two independent truths: whether a valid loop range exists, and whether looping is currently enabled for that range.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: After setting both A and B on a video, the A-B loop checkbox becomes enabled and checked.
Unchecking the A-B loop checkbox disables looping without deleting the stored A/B points.
Checking it again re-enables looping for the stored valid range.
knownIssues: This slice does not yet change image slider behavior, slideshow, or right-stick volume.
The generated scene JSON remains more verbose than a VaM-saved scene file.
